title: The Talking Oak by Lord Alfred Tennyson
content:
Once more the gate behind me falls;
Once more before my face
I see the moulder'd Abbey-walls,
That stand within the chace.

Beyond the lodge the city

title: To E. Fitzgerald: Tiresias by Lord Alfred Tennyson
content:
OLD FITZ, who from your suburb grange,
Where once I tarried for a while,
Glance at the wheeling orb of change,
And greet it with a kindly smile;
Whom

title: The Grandmother by Lord Alfred Tennyson
content:
I.
And Willy, my eldest-born, is gone, you say, little Anne?
Ruddy and white, and strong on his legs, he looks like a man.
And Willy's wife has writte

title: A Farewell by Lord Alfred Tennyson
content:
Flow down, cold rivulet, to the sea,
     Thy tribute wave deliver:
No more by thee my steps shall be,
     For ever and for ever.

Flow, softly flow,

title: In Memoriam A. H. H.: 7. Dark house, by which once more I s by Lord Alfred Tennyson
content:
Dark house, by which once more I stand
Here in the long unlovely street,
Doors, where my heart was used to beat
So quickly, waiting for a hand,
A hand
